_comment: Say It — video QC rubric. Drives both the deterministic ffmpeg checks (exact, no judgment) and the Claude vision checks (judgment a diff can't make). Numbers here are calibrated to the ACTUAL renders in out/films (4K h264, ~4-5 Mbps, AAC 384k), not the aspirational 45 Mbps/20 min from the prototype. Edit thresholds here, not in the harness code.

runtime._comment: The strong check: render duration vs the sum of slide durations in this lesson's own JSON. That is the source of truth for THIS lesson, not a fixed target.

curriculum_rules._framing_note: Words allowed in title/score framing that are not part of the taught set. They downgrade a vocabulary hit from FAIL to WARN instead of failing the gate — near-universal cognates.

illustration_style: European clean-line comic: bold, uniform-weight ink outlines, flat solid color fills, no gradient shading, no soft drop shadows, no photo-real rendering. Flag any frame that breaks from this.

frame_sampling._comment: One frame per content slide, taken at the slide's midpoint. Sample times come from the lesson JSON timeline, so each frame is checked against the EXACT word/translation/phase that should be on screen at that moment.

_vision_model_note: Escalate hard cases to claude-opus-4-8 by editing this value.
